acceptedAnswer.text: CrowAgent is a compliance software platform that helps UK organisations manage MEES energy efficiency, PPN 002 social value, and CSRD sustainability reporting. We convert complex regulatory requirements into automated, self-serve tools that deliver compliance outputs in under 10 minutes.

Visit app.crowagent.ai/signup to create your account. Every plan includes a 14-day free trial with no credit card required. You can start analysing properties or scoring bids immediately after signing up.

Yes. Every paid plan includes a 14-day free trial. You get full access to all features during the trial period. No credit card is required to start. The CSRD Checker is always free with no account needed.

MEES (Minimum Energy Efficiency Standards) is UK legislation under SI 2015/962 that sets minimum EPC requirements for commercial properties. Currently, properties must reach EPC Band E to grant or renew a lease. A proposed Band C target for 2028 is under consultation but is not yet enacted law.

Band C 2028 is a proposed regulatory target that would require all commercial properties to achieve EPC Band C by April 2028. This is subject to legislative confirmation and is not yet current law. CrowAgent Core helps you understand your position under both current Band E rules and the proposed 2028 target so you can plan ahead.

mainEntity.name: What properties are affected by MEES?

acceptedAnswer.text: MEES applies to all privately rented commercial properties in England and Wales that are legally required to have an EPC. This includes offices, retail units, industrial premises, and warehouses. Properties rated F or G are currently non-compliant, while D and E rated properties would need improvement under the proposed 2028 Band C target.

Penalties are calculated using the rateable value formula from SI 2015/962 regulation 39. For breaches under 3 months: 10% of rateable value (min GBP 5,000, max GBP 50,000). For breaches of 3 months or more: 20% of rateable value (min GBP 10,000, max GBP 150,000). CrowAgent never uses a flat GBP 30,000 estimate. We always calculate from the actual rateable value.

PPN 002 (Procurement Policy Note 002) is UK government policy requiring a minimum 10% social value weighting on all central government contract evaluations. It applies to all procurements above the relevant thresholds and requires suppliers to demonstrate social value commitments using the TOMs (Themes, Outcomes, and Measures) framework.

Under PPN 002, at least 10% of the total evaluation score for central government contracts must be allocated to social value. Many local authorities and NHS trusts apply the same or higher thresholds. CrowMark helps you consistently hit this threshold with automated scoring and AI-generated narratives.

Social value is scored using the TOMs framework with proxy values from the Oxford Social Value Bank (2023-24 edition). Each measure has a monetary proxy value per unit. Your total social value is calculated as: SUM(proxy_value x quantity x duration_months). CrowMark automates this calculation using the latest Oxford SVB data stored in our database.
CrowAgent Core has Free, Starter, Pro, Portfolio, and Agency tiers. CrowMark has Free, Solo, Team, and Agency tiers. All paid plans include a 14-day free trial and annual billing saves 10%.

Yes. You can cancel your subscription at any time from Settings > Billing in the platform. Your access continues until the end of the current billing period. Your data is preserved, so if you resubscribe later, everything will still be there.

Subscriptions are billed monthly or annually (10% discount). Your billing period starts from the date you subscribe. After your 14-day trial, you will be prompted to choose a plan. If you don't subscribe, your data is preserved but you won't be able to add new properties or create new contracts.

EPC data comes from the MHCLG (Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government) Energy Performance of Buildings register, the official UK government source. Social value proxy values come from the Oxford Social Value Bank 2023-24 edition. Financial modelling uses HM Treasury Green Book 3.5% discount rate and Ofgem 2% energy escalation rate.

Yes. We use Supabase (PostgreSQL) with Row Level Security on all tables. All data is encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest. We are protected by Cloudflare (DDoS, Bot Fight Mode, SSL Full Strict). Authentication uses Supabase Auth with optional TOTP MFA. We are ICO registered and GDPR compliant.

mainEntity.name: How accurate is the EPC data?

acceptedAnswer.text: We fetch directly from the MHCLG EPC register, which is the same data source used by energy assessors and local authorities. Our system caches data for 90 days and falls back to cached results if the register is temporarily unavailable. If no EPC is found, you can enter property data manually for analysis.
